title: Sheet templates: trace / fade / copy / free-practice
context: A single trace sheet is sufficient for the M0 pilot, but a pedagogically useful practice set requires four row types: trace rows (full guide + glyph for tracing), fade-to-blank rows (glyph fades across the row so the learner progressively writes more independently), copy rows (model glyph shown once, rest blank), and free-practice rows (guidelines only). These compose with paper sizes and accessibility variants. The SheetTemplate schema entity must be implemented in the renderer to produce all four row types.
objective: Implement the four SheetTemplate row types (trace, fade-to-blank, copy, free-practice) in the renderer, each configurable for paper size (A4 and US Letter) and compatible with the accessibility variants from a11y-011, with deterministic SVG output and golden-file tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Trace row renders the glyph at full opacity with guideline overlay, repeated for a configurable number of repetitions.
Fade-to-blank row renders the glyph at progressively decreasing opacity across the row (configurable steps) until the final cells are guidelines-only.
Copy row renders the model glyph once at the start of the row; the remaining cells are guidelines-only for free copying.
Free-practice row renders guidelines only (no glyph) for unrestricted practice.
All four templates produce correct output at A4 and US Letter paper sizes with correct margins.
Templates compose cleanly with the accessibility variants (a11y-011) without layout breakage.
Golden-file SVG tests for each template type; determinism preserved; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md section 5 (Sheet templates — trace, fade-to-blank, copy, free-practice rows)
output: Four SheetTemplate implementations in packages/core, with golden-file SVG tests for each — committed via PR with DCO sign-off.
